revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:帝国大学令]]
'''Teikoku Daigakurei''' (帝国大学令, {{oldstyle|帝󠄁國大學令}}) was an [[imperial ordinance]] regulating [[higher education institutions]] in [[prewar Japan]] (the {{ill|Empire of Japan|en|Empire of Japan|qid=Q188712|lt=Empire of Japan|1=Empire of Japan}}) known as the {{ill|Imperial Universities|en|Imperial Universities|qid=Q562092|lt=Imperial Universities|1=Imperial Universities}}. Two versions of this ordinance existed: * '''Teikoku Daigakurei''' (Imperial Ordinance No. 3 of March 1, Meiji 19) - Promulgated in {{ill|1886|en|1886|qid=Q7822|lt=1886|1=1886}}, referred to as the [[#First Teikoku Daigakurei|First Teikoku Daigakurei]]. * '''Teikoku Daigakurei''' (Imperial Ordinance No. 12 of February 7, Taisho 8) - A complete revision in {{ill|1918|en|1918|qid=Q2094|lt=1918|1=1918}}, referred to as the [[#Second Teikoku Daigakurei|Second Teikoku Daigakurei]]. Renamed in {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} to the '''Kokuritsu Sōgō Daigakurei''' (国立総合大学令, {{oldstyle|國立總合大學令}}), it was repealed by the [[National School Establishment Act]] of {{ill|1949|en|1949|qid=Q5188|lt=1949|1=1949}}.
== Overview == During the {{ill|First Itō Cabinet|en|First Itō Cabinet|qid=Q1045014|lt=First Itō Cabinet|1=First Itō cabinet}}, the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]], {{ill|Mori Arinori|en|Mori Arinori|qid=Q1194472|lt=Mori Arinori|1=Mori Arinori}}, oversaw the legislative process that replaced the [[Kyōikurei]] with a series of ordinances collectively known as the [[Gakkōrei]]. Among these was the Imperial University Ordinance (Imperial Ordinance No. 3 of March 1, Meiji 19), promulgated in [[March 1886]] alongside the [[Shihan Gakkōrei]], [[Shōgakkōrei]], and [[Chūgakkōrei]].
Following the dissolution of the [[Kōbushō]] (Ministry of Public Works), the [[Kōbu Daigakkō]] was transferred to the [[:en:Ministry of Education|Ministry of Education]] in December of the previous year. This ordinance formally merged it into the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|qid=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}, which was reconstituted as the sole "Imperial University" ([[:en:Teikoku Daigaku|Teikoku Daigaku]]). It consisted of five [[:en:Faculties|Faculties]] (法科, 医科, 工科, 文科, 理科), an [[:en:Graduate School|Graduate Department]], and administrative roles such as the {{ill|President|en|President|qid=Q1175634|lt=President|1=President}} (a [[Shinninkan|Chokuninkan]] a11ointee). By {{ill|1889|en|1889|qid=Q7831|lt=1889|1=1889}}, a Faculty of Agriculture was added.
The ordinance was significantly revised in {{ill|1893|en|1893|qid=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}}, introducing faculty councils and formalizing the lecture system. Subsequent imperial universities (e.g., [[:en:Kyoto Imperial University|Kyoto Imperial University]]) established from {{ill|1897|en|1897|qid=Q7847|lt=1897|1=1897}} onward were regulated separately under additional ordinances. A complete overhaul in {{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}} (Taisho 8) replaced the faculty-based structure with [[:en:Colleges|Colleges]] within a unified [[:en:comprehensive university|comprehensive university]] system.
The ordinance was renamed in {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} under postwar [[educational reforms]] and repealed in {{ill|1949|en|1949|qid=Q5188|lt=1949|1=1949}} with the enactment of the [[National School Establishment Act]], transitioning Imperial Universities into the modern [[:en:National Universities|National Universities]].
== First Teikoku Daigakurei == {{Japan Law | Title=Teikoku Daigakurei | Status=Obsolete | Type=[[Education Law]] | Ministry=[[:en:Ministry of Education|Ministry of Education]] | Content=[[Imperial University]] regulations | Related=[[Shihan Gakkōrei]], [[Shōgakkōrei]], [[Chūgakkōrei]], [[Kōtō Gakkōrei]], [[University Ordinance]] | Link=[{{NDLDC|2944009/1}} Official Gazette March 2, 1886] | Wikisource=帝國大學令 (公布時) }} Promulgated on [[March 2, 1886]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 3 of Meiji 19) and implemented on [[April 1, 1886]].
=== Provisions === ;Purpose (Article 1) Defines the purpose of Imperial Universities as institutions for higher learning and research. ;Structure (Articles 2–4) * [[:en:Faculties|Faculties]] - Award diplomas upon Completion tier 2]] - Provides advanced education for faculty graduates, awarding degrees upon Completion tier 2]]) ** Ensures university order and reports to the [[:en:Minister of Education|Minister of Education]]. ** Chairs faculty councils and supervises university operations. * Councillors ** Selected from [[:en:Professors|Professors]] by the Minister of Education for five-year terms, responsible for policy deliberations. * Administrative Staff (clerks and secretaries a11ointed by the Ministry). ;Faculties (Article 10) 1. Faculty of Law (Departments of Law and Political Science) 2. Faculty of Medicine 3. Faculty of Engineering 4. Faculty of Letters 5. Faculty of Science ;Faculty Personnel (Articles 11–14) * Deans, professors, assistant professors, tutors, and administrative staff.
== Second Teikoku Daigakurei == {{Japan Law | Title=National Comprehensive University Ordinance | Alias={{Japan Law/Alias|Q118385174}} | Number={{Japan Law/Number|Q118385174}} | Status=Obsolete | Type=[[Education Law]] | Ministry=[[:en:Ministry of Education|Ministry of Education]] | Content={{ill|Imperial Universities|en|Imperial Universities|qid=Q562092|lt=Imperial Universities (National Comprehensive Universities)|1=Imperial Universities}} regulations | Related=[[University Ordinance]], [[School Education Act]], [[National School Establishment Act]] | Link=[{{NDLDC|2954067/2}} Official Gazette February 7, 1919] | Wikisource=帝國大學令改正ノ件 }} The ordinance was entirely revised on [[February 7, 1919]] (Imperial Ordinance No. 12 of Taisho 8), effective [[April 1, 1919]]. It adopted a collegiate structure consistent with the new [[University Ordinance]], replacing the Faculty system with unified Colleges.
=== Changes === * Faculty assemblies were formalized, and councils included elected representatives. * The term "Faculty" was replaced with "College," with each university consisting of multiple Colleges as specified by individual ordinances.
=== Renaming and Repeal === * {{ill|1947|en|1947|qid=Q5263|lt=1947|1=1947}} - Renamed "National Comprehensive University Ordinance" (Government Ordinance No. 204 of September 30, Showa 22). * {{ill|1949|en|1949|qid=Q5188|lt=1949|1=1949}} - Repealed under the [[National School Establishment Act]] (Law No. 150 of Showa 24).
== See Also == *[[:en:Professor Emeritus|Professor Emeritus]]
== External Links == {{Wikisource|帝國大學令 (公布時)|帝国大学令 (Imperial Ordinance No. 3 of Meiji 19)}} {{Wikisource|帝國大學令改正ノ件 (Imperial Ordinance No. 12 of Taisho 8)}} * [http://www.geocities.jp/nakanolib/rei/rm19-3.htm Imperial University Ordinance (Meiji 19)] (Archived) * [http://www.geocities.jp/nakanolib/rei/rt08-12.htm Imperial University Ordinance (Taisho 8)] (Archived)
